BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". 82	/// Sub-assertion within a case: run bash snippet, check exit code.
83	struct RbtdrfSub {
84	    label: &'static str,
85	    setup: &'static str,
86	    command: &'static str,
87	    expect_ok: bool,
88	}
89	
90	impl RbtdrfSub {
91	    const fn ok(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
92	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: true }
93	    }
94	    const fn fatal(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
95	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: false }
96	    }
97	    const fn ok_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
98	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: true }
99	    }
100	    const fn fatal_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
101	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: false }
102	    }
103	}

105	/// Run a bash script, return (exit_code, stdout, stderr). Saves traces to case dir.
106	fn rbtdrf_run_bash(
107	    project_root: &Path,
108	    script: &str,
109	    dir: &Path,
110	    trace_prefix: &str,
111	) -> Result<(i32, String, String), String> {
112	    let output = Command::new(rbtdri_bash_program())
113	        .arg("-c")
114	        .arg(script)
115	        .current_dir(project_root)
116	        .output()
117	        .map_err(|e| format!("bash execution failed: {}", e))?;
118	
119	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned();
120	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
121	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
122	
123	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-script.sh", trace_prefix)), script);
124	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", trace_prefix)), &stdout);
125	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", trace_prefix)), &stderr);
126	
127	    Ok((code, stdout, stderr))
128	}

130	/// Run enrollment-validation sub-assertions against BUV.
131	fn rbtdrf_run_ev(
132	    dir: &Path,
133	    enrollment: &str,
134	    subs: &[RbtdrfSub],
135	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
136	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
137	        Ok(r) => r,
138	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
139	    };
140	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
141	
142	    for (i, sub) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
143	        let script = format!(
144	            "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n{}\n{}\n{}",
145	            rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
146	            enrollment,
147	            sub.setup,
148	            sub.command,
149	        );
150	
151	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
152	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
153	                let ok = code == 0;
154	                if ok != sub.expect_ok {
155	                    return if sub.expect_ok {
156	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", sub.label, code))
157	                    } else {
158	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", sub.label))
159	                    };
160	                }
161	            }
162	            Err(e) => {
163	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", sub.label, e));
164	            }
165	        }
166	    }
167	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
168	}

170	/// Drive a regime's public `*_probate` function against a synthetic
171	/// baseline+override written to the case temp dir. `expect_ok` selects the
172	/// verdict polarity: pass-anchors validate the pristine baseline (true);
173	/// negatives apply one violating override and expect non-zero (false).
174	/// Sources only the contract surface — `buv_validation.sh` plus the one regime
175	/// module — mirroring the `buv_vet` bridge that enrollment-validation uses: no
176	/// internal-module chain, no z-private reach.
177	///
178	/// `tools_subdir` is the repo-relative directory holding the module
179	/// ("Tools/rbk" or "Tools/buk"). `prelude` is raw bash staged after buv's
180	/// kindle but before the regime module's probate — for regimes whose kindle or
181	/// enforce reaches a neighbor (rbrp → rbgc's payor regex; burs → bubc, whose
182	/// constants its enrollment help-string expands). Relative paths resolve
183	/// against cwd (the repo root, set by rbtdrf_run_bash). Empty for the common
184	/// case, mirroring how rbtdrf_rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix stages rbgc in-harness.
185	fn rbtdrf_run_probate_in(
186	    dir: &Path,
187	    tools_subdir: &str,
188	    module: &str,
189	    probate_fn: &str,
190	    prelude: &str,
191	    baseline: &str,
192	    override_: &str,
193	    expect_ok: bool,
194	    label: &str,
195	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
196	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
197	        Ok(r) => r,
198	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
199	    };
200	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
201	    let module_path = root.join(tools_subdir).join(module);
202	
203	    let env_file = dir.join(format!("{}.env", label));
204	    let body = format!("{}\n{}\n", baseline, override_);
205	    if let Err(e) = std::fs::write(&env_file, &body) {
206	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: write env failed: {}", label, e));
207	    }
208	
209	    let script = format!(
210	        "set -euo pipefail\n\
211	         source '{}'\n\
212	         source '{}'\n\
213	         zbuv_kindle\n\
214	         {}\
215	         {} '{}'",
216	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
217	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&module_path),
218	        prelude,
219	        probate_fn,
220	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&env_file),
221	    );
222	
223	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, label) {
224	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
225	            let ok = code == 0;
226	            if ok == expect_ok {
227	                rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
228	            } else if expect_ok {
229	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code))
230	            } else {
231	                rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", label))
232	            }
233	        }
234	        Err(e) => rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
235	    }
236	}

238	/// Common-case wrapper: RBK module, no prereqs. Preserves the call shape used
239	/// by the reference rv_ quartet (rbrr/rbrd/rbrv/rbrn).

569	// --- Reference types ---
570	
571	const RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST: &str =
572	    "sha256:abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789";
573	
574	fn rbtdrf_ev_odref_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
575	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
576	        Ok(r) => r,
577	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
578	    };
579	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
580	    let enrollment = format!(
581	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
582	         buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"References\"\n\
583	         buv_odref_enroll TEST_IMAGE \"Container image\"",
584	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
585	    );
586	    let d = RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST;
587	
588	    let subs: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
589	        ("standard registry", &format!("docker.io/library/alpine@{}", d)),
590	        ("multi-level repo", &format!("us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-proj/my-repo/tool@{}", d)),
591	        ("registry with port", &format!("registry.local:5000/myimage@{}", d)),
592	    ];
593	
594	    for (i, (label, image)) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
595	        let script = format!("{}\nexport TEST_IMAGE=\"{}\"\nbuv_vet \"TEST\"", enrollment, image);
596	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
597	            Ok((0, _, _)) => {}
598	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
599	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code));
600	            }
601	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
602	        }
603	    }
604	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
605	}
